title: Johanna Meehan Oral History Interview for the LGBTQ+ Oral Histories of Central Iowa Project
description: Johanna Meehan (1956-2024) taught in the Philosophy Department at Grinnell College and was a proud lesbian and queer parent. In this interview, she discusses living in Central Iowa, meeting and marrying her partner of 40 years, Maura, and raising their two children. She talks about the challenges she faced coming out to her family, experiences of parenting her children, and interactions she has had in Grinnell. She also covers her love of teaching and interactions with her students.

title: Rick Miller Oral History Interview for the LGBTQ+ Oral Histories of Central Iowa Project
description: Rick discusses his upbringing in Sac City, Iowa, his experiences growing up gay, and his journey through college at the University of Northern Iowa. He reflects on his involvement in the LGBT community in Des Moines, including the impact of Anita Bryant's visit, the formation of various LGBT organizations, and the development of the gay bar scene. Rick also shares personal stories about his relationships, the AIDS epidemic, and his work with the Des Moines Gay Men's Chorus and other community groups.
